skill.name: test-ops
skill.description: Standardizes test planning, implementation, execution, and reporting workflows.
skill.summary: Guides teams through structured test design, mocking strategy, execution checks, and test reporting.
skill.author: A-Genno

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive: the line is a Japanese description, not code or cryptographic usage. The entropy finding at SKILL.md line 1 is also a false positive caused by UTF-8 Japanese text and frontmatter; no obfuscation, executable code, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent test planning and reporting can hide quality gaps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for test design, mocking, execution checks, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Defines test design principles for normal, error, boundary, and edge cases.
Guides mock strategies for databases, external APIs, time-dependent logic, and file I/O.
Outlines a step-by-step workflow from feature analysis to coverage review.
Promotes clear test names, readable assertions, and useful failure messages.
Encourages independent tests that can run without relying on other test cases.
limitations: Does not include executable test scripts or framework-specific commands.
Does not inspect a repository unless the assistant is given project files.
Does not guarantee 100 percent coverage without project-specific implementation work.
Uses Japanese source guidance, so teams may need localized output preferences.

use_cases.title: Plan Release Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Create a structured test plan that covers expected behavior, failures, boundaries, and edge cases before release.

use_cases.title: Design Maintainable Unit Tests
use_cases.description: Convert feature behavior into independent test cases with clear setup, action, and assertion phases.

use_cases.title: Prepare Test Execution Reports
use_cases.description: Summarize executed cases, failures, coverage status, and follow-up actions in a consistent format.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a single feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-ops skill to create a test plan for this feature. Include normal cases, error cases, boundary values, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design Mocking Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-ops skill to design a mocking strategy for database access, external APIs, time-dependent logic, and file I/O in this module.

prompt_templates.title: Review Test Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-ops skill to review these tests for independence, readability, maintainability, assertion clarity, and failure message quality.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Release Test Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-ops skill to summarize test execution results, coverage gaps, failed scenarios, risk areas, and required follow-up work.

output_examples.input: A user registration feature needs test planning before implementation.
output_examples.output: A test plan grouped by normal registration, validation failures, duplicate accounts, boundary inputs, and external dependency behavior.

output_examples.input: A payment workflow uses a database, third-party API, scheduled retry, and receipt file output.
output_examples.output: A mocking plan that separates repository fixtures, HTTP response patterns, fixed time values, and virtual file system checks.

output_examples.input: A team needs a weekly test execution summary for a release candidate.
output_examples.output: A concise report with executed scope, pass and fail status, unresolved defects, coverage notes, and recommended next actions.

best_practices: Start with feature analysis before writing individual test cases.
Keep tests independent so failures identify the real affected behavior.
Write assertions and failure messages that explain the expected business outcome.
anti_patterns: Do not change production code only to make a test pass.
Do not rely on live external services when mocks or fixtures can cover the behavior.
Do not treat coverage percentage as a substitute for meaningful scenario coverage.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure test planning, implementation, execution checks, mocking decisions, and reporting.

faq.question: Does it write executable tests by itself?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and structure. Executable tests require project files, frameworks, and implementation context.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill framework-specific?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is general and can be adapted to different test frameworks.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access exists in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: Why were static security findings dismissed?
faq.answer: The flagged lines contain Japanese frontmatter and prose, not cryptographic code or obfuscated executable content.
